IN THE AMOUNT OF $892: "ANY PARTY MAY OBTAIN DISCOVERY REGARDING ANY MATTER, NOT PRIVILEGED, THAT IS RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER INVOLVED IN THE PENDING ACTION OR TO THE DETERMINATION OF ANY MOTION MADE IN THAT ACTION, IF THE MATTER EITHER IS ITSELF ADMISSIBLE IN EVIDENCE OR APPEARS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE." CAL. CIV. PROC. CODE 2017.010 (CLEANED UP). PLAINTIFFS HAVE ESTABLISHED THE RELEVANCE OF THE DISCOVERY THEY SEEK. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR DOCUMENTS IS LIMITED TO THE TIME PERIOD JULY 1, 2012 TO THE PRESENT. THE CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS FOR SOPHISTICATED HOME BUILDER, INC. MAY REDACT FROM THE RECORDS THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS' PERSONAL PAYMENT SOURCE INFORMATION, INCLUDING BANK ACCOUNT NUMBERS, PAYMENT INSTITUTION, CREDIT CARD ACCOUNT INFORMATION, OR SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBERS, BUT MAY NOT REDACT THE AMOUNT OF ANY INVOICES OR ANY THIRD-PARTY PAYMENT SOURCE INFORMATION IF THE INVOICES WERE PAID BY PERSONS OR ENTITIES OTHER THAN DEFENDANTS. SOPHISTICATED HOME BUILDER, INC. SHALL PROVIDE WRITTEN RESPONSES, RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, AND, IF NECESSARY, A PRIVILEGE LOG WITHIN 20 CALENDAR DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF A FINAL ORDER ON THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: NAOMI GRAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel; Documents From Sophisticated Home Builder, Inc.; Request For Sanctions Against Sophisticated Home Builder, Inc. In The Amount Of $892
Off Calendar Apr-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mary Catherine Wiederhold, esq. (415-533-0735) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Not argued. Counsel presents proposed order. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs WENDY HOUGH and MATTHEW HRUDKA's Motion To Compel; Documents From Sophisticated Home Builder, Inc.; Request For Sanctions Against Sophisticated Home Builder, Inc. In The Amount Of $892:

"Any party may obtain discovery regarding any matter, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action or to the determination of any motion made in that action, if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence." Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2017.010 (cleaned up). Plaintiffs have established the relevance of the discovery they seek. Plaintiffs' motion is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiffs' request for documents is limited to the time period July 1, 2012 to the present. The custodian of records for Sophisticated Home Builder, Inc. may redact from the records the individual Defendants' personal payment source information, including bank account numbers, payment institution, credit card account information, or Social Security numbers, but may not redact the amount of any invoices or any third-party payment source information if the invoices were paid by persons or entities other than Defendants. Sophisticated Home Builder, Inc. shall provide written responses, responsive documents, and, if necessary, a privilege log within 20 calendar days of notice of entry of a final order on this motion. Plaintiffs' motion is otherwise DENIED. The request for sanctions is DENIED.

Order is signed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Requessts For Documents, Set One Number 1-45; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Christopher Scorfina And His Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $1,717
Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq., Telephone 415-533-0733, appearing for Plaintiff
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS WENDY HOUGH, MATTHEW HRUDKA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Requessts For Documents, Set One Number 1-45; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Christopher Scorfina And His Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $1,717.

The Motion is granted in part and denied in part. CCP section 2031.310(g) provides that the court shall limit the extent of discovery of ESI if the information sought can be obtained more conveniently from another source or if the discovery sought is unreasonably cumulative or duplicative. This court cannot find any equivalent Code section that applies specifically to documents that are not stored electronically, but believes CCP section 2031.310(g) is applicable here by analogy. Here, Mr. Scorfina's responses, Bates stamped DEF 01- DEF0271, are alleged to be identical to his co-defendant's, Mr. Courage. However, his written responses and verification do not specifically state that his responses are identical to Mr. Courage's. Plaintiff is entitled to a verified response which states this fact, if true. To the extent, Mr. Scorfina's documents are not identical in all respects to Mr. Courage's, then he is obligated to produce such non-identical documents. For example, if Mr. Scorfina has similar documents with margin notes or other differences, then such documents are not identical and the request is not cumulative. Mr. Scorfina shall, within 10 days, serve a verification stating, if true, that his only responsive documents (DEF 01- DEF0271) are identical in all respects to those produced by Mr. Courage. Within that time period, he must also serve responsive non identical documents, if any.

It is not entirely clear from the pleadings whether other responses are at issue.

No sanctions. Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
